data.body:
G4 container child-consistency audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child fe57 "Hermetic-but-honest Jira fixtures + verified fake client served through the production path"), all 7 cross-child interaction modes checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937) under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter, corroborated against the AS-SHIPPED repo. VERDICT: clean pairing — NO G4 cross-child finding; findings list EMPTY.

REPO RE-VERIFICATION (this pass):
- fe57's fixtures shipped: tests/fixtures/jira/ holds search.json + comment_map.json + issuelinks_map.json + parent_map.json (+ _meta.json) — the exact AC1 set (search + comment + issuelinks + parent).
- The FakeAcliClient shipped at tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/jira_contract/_fakes.py:31 with EXACTLY the four real-client signatures: search_issues(jql, start_at=0, max_results=50) (L50), get_comment_map(project, jql=None) (L56), get_issuelinks_map (L59), get_parent_map (L62) — each returning a deep copy of the fixture "verbatim, with NO shape massaging" (docstring L37-38, AC2/AC4). It installs via the established seam (patch fetcher._load_acli → stub module whose AcliClient(**kwargs) factory yields the fake, L66-79), so fixtures flow through the production parent/comment/issuelinks enrichment merge — the bug-0ee6/3f04 seam (docstring L11-17).
- RELOCATION is clean: the fake landed in the jira_contract/ subdir alongside its three consumers (test_snapshot_contract.py [8937], test_verified_fake_contract.py [59ef], test_roundtrip.py [822a]); all import it via same-directory `from _fakes import ...`. A repo search for the old module path `rebar_reconciler._fakes` returns NO matches — the path move introduced no dangling/residual import.

7-MODE WALK (parent × fe57):
- Implicit shared state — BENIGN. fe57 PRODUCES the fixtures + FakeAcliClient; 8937/59ef/822a CONSUME (read) them over declared depends_on fe57 edges. No sibling co-writes tests/fixtures/jira/ or _fakes.py; clean producer/consumer split, no shared mutable write. Disjoint from the outbound-applier cluster (33d0/2359 → apply_outbound.py/batch_dispatch.py/applier.py/reconcile.py) and from 8937's schema/fetcher.py edits.
- Conflicting assumptions — BENIGN. fe57 AC2 ("fake serves fixtures through production fetch_snapshot enrichment with NO shape transformation") is mutually reinforcing with 59ef AC2 (the fake FAILS the contract if its return shape diverges from the real client) and 8937's snapshot-entry schema (which targets exactly those unmassaged shapes). The fake's surface mirrors the real-client signatures the siblings contract against. fe57 is purely INBOUND enrichment; it neither asserts nor depends on the outbound labels_applied representation that is the epic's one real cross-child seam (correctly localized to the 33d0↔2359 outbound-telemetry pairing, resolved as-shipped via integer counters). No representation contradiction.
- Dependency gap — BENIGN. fe57 is the root foundation; it needs no internal input and correctly declares no depends_on. Its only external dependency (live Jira for capture) is correctly live-gated per AC3. Its OUT items (the contract test → story B/8937; the verified-fake honesty check → story C/59ef) are present as named siblings. No missing input edge.
- Scope overlap — BENIGN. fe57 owns fixtures + fake + capture/scrub script + re-capture doc and explicitly scopes OUT the consuming contract test (→8937) and the verified-fake fidelity harness (→59ef). 59ef VERIFIES the fake fe57 BUILDS; 8937 CONSUMES the fixtures fe57 CAPTURES; 822a reuses them for the round-trip — complementary, not duplicative. Distinct surface from every sibling.
- Ordering violation — BENIGN. The only hard ordering edge (foundation before its consumers) is captured by the three declared depends_on fe57 edges, consistent with the epic's "A → B first" sequencing. fe57's files are disjoint from the 33d0↔2359 same-region outbound-applier sequencing cluster, so it is not entangled in that hazard.
- Consumer impact — BENIGN. fe57's deliverables are additive test scaffolding. AC4 (consumed ONLY via the production code path; no test hand-reshapes) and the re-capture doc ("so fixtures stay honest and do not rot into change-detectors") support the epic's anti-change-detector posture / parent "no net increase in brittle tests" rather than imposing brittle expectations. Secret-scrubbing (AC1/AC3 + the committed secret-scan test test_jira_fixtures.py) keeps committed fixtures safe for every consumer.
- Residual references — BENIGN. All four faked method signatures exist on the real client and are routed through the production fetch_snapshot merge (verified). 33d0 deletes the DEAD OUTBOUND typed-leaf applier, NOT the inbound enrichment surface fe57 exercises, so its deletion strands nothing fe57 references. The _fakes.py relocation into jira_contract/ left no dangling import (confirmed: zero matches for the old module path; consumers use same-dir imports).

The roster-level parent anti-change-detector-guardrail criterion is a G3 COVERAGE matter (and per the parent AC's verbatim "(owned by story B)" clause is owned by 8937) — not a G4 cross-child inconsistency, so not addressed here. fe57's own re-capture doc is self-scoped to its fixtures, consistent with that.

VERDICT for this pairing (parent × fe57): no G4 cross-child finding; all 7 interaction modes read benign under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter. Findings list EMPTY.
